metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (with a proving grep command) for what AGENTS.md must contain, or remove it from the Files list if no content change is needed.
evidence: 'AGENTS.md' appears in the Files list but is absent from all four ACs and from the Plan Corrections assertion block
No grep or pytest command targets AGENTS.md
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section but has no acceptance criterion, no assertion, and no proving command. An executor cannot know what to write there or how to verify the change is correct.
findings.location: Files: docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)
scenarios: Executor adds something to AGENTS.md, reviewer has no way to verify correctness
Executor skips AGENTS.md entirely — there is no failing check to catch that
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] AGENTS.md documents the `[metrics]` extra and PATH prereqs for contributors. PROVE: `grep -q "\[metrics\]" AGENTS.md`' OR remove AGENTS.md from the Files list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or subsume into an existing one) that specifies what AGENTS.md must contain and provides a PROVE command, or remove AGENTS.md from the Files list if it requires no change.
evidence: Files lists 'AGENTS.md' explicitly
No AC references AGENTS.md or contains a PROVE command targeting it
ABSENCE: scanning all four ACs, none mention AGENTS.md
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section as a deliverable but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify its content — it is an orphan deliverable with no observable done-definition.
findings.location: Files section: 'docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)'
scenarios: Implementer may skip AGENTS.md updates or make inconsistent changes with no enforcement
Reviewer has no observable criterion to accept or reject the AGENTS.md change

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with content spec and proof command) for AGENTS.md, or remove it from the Files list if no update is actually needed.
evidence: Plan Files line: 'docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)'
No AC entry references AGENTS.md; all four ACs address the other four files only.
ABSENCE: no proof command like `grep -q ... AGENTS.md` exists anywhere in the ACs or Plan corrections.
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion, no specified content guidance, and no proof command — an implementer has no enforceable target for what to write there.
findings.location: Files list: `AGENTS.md`; Acceptance criteria section (no AC covering AGENTS.md)
scenarios: Implementer writes nothing to AGENTS.md (file listed but no AC enforces it), leaving agent/developer onboarding guidance stale.
Implementer writes inconsistent or contradictory guidance to AGENTS.md that conflicts with the other three docs, with no test catching the divergence.
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add an AC such as 'AGENTS.md documents the three-part metrics install for agent context. PROVE: `grep -q "metrics" AGENTS.md`' and include a corresponding assertion in `test_metrics_docs.py`; or (b) if AGENTS.md requires only a brief cross-reference, document that explicitly and drop the file from the Files list if no substantive change is planned.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'e.g. "no analyzer"' with 'the exact phrase "no analyzer"' (or update the test assertion to use a regex covering both forms) so the required string is unambiguous.
evidence: 'an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")' — 'e.g.' implies the string is an example, not a requirement
Plan Corrections test assertion for user-guide.md checks for the string 'no analyzer' specifically
findings.finding: The Unavailable-fallback phrase for user-guide.md is described as 'e.g. "no analyzer"', making it ambiguous whether 'no analyzer' is the required exact string — yet the Plan Corrections test assertion checks for that specific string. An executor who uses a different phrasing (e.g. 'Unavailable') will produce readable docs but a failing test.
findings.location: Plan Corrections — AC4 assertion: 'an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")'
scenarios: Executor writes 'When no analyzer is available, the metric shows Unavailable' — test looks for 'no analyzer', may or may not pass depending on case sensitivity
Executor writes 'metric is Unavailable if the tool is missing' — readable but test fails
findings.suggested_fix: Change to: 'must contain the phrase "no analyzer" (exact string checked by the test)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the exact fallback phrase the new `test_metrics_surface_documented` assertion must check (e.g. mandate `"Unavailable"` with capital-U to match the CLI output vocabulary, or explicitly state it reuses the pre-existing `"unavailable"` check and the new assertion should not duplicate it).
evidence: AC4 text: 'docs/user-guide.md contains "rebar[metrics]" AND an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")'

Pre-existing assertion in test_metrics_docs.py line 36: `assert "unavailable" in text` already covers user-guide.md — the new assertion's vocabulary ('no analyzer' vs 'unavailable') is never reconciled.
ABSENCE: no fixed string is mandated, only an illustrative example; the enum value for the fallback phrase is never pinned.
findings.finding: The 'Unavailable-fallback phrase' that the new `test_metrics_surface_documented` test must assert is specified only as `e.g. "no analyzer"` — an illustrative example rather than a fixed string. This leaves the assertion vocabulary undefined: the implementer may choose any phrase, which may conflict with or duplicate the pre-existing `assert "unavailable" in text` in `test_user_guide_has_metrics_section`, or produce a green test that does not actually enforce the intended fallback contract.
findings.location: Plan corrections (round 4) — AC4 ENUMERATED ASSERTIONS, assertion (3): 'an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")'
scenarios: Implementer writes `assert 'no analyzer' in text` — this is a NEW string requirement on user-guide.md beyond the pre-existing 'unavailable' check, silently doubling the documentation constraint without the author realising.
Implementer writes `assert 'unavailable' in text` — the new assertion is redundant with the pre-existing one and provides no incremental enforcement, defeating the purpose of the new AC.
Implementer chooses a third phrase — the plan's stated contract is not verifiable and the test may pass while the doc says something different.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `e.g. "no analyzer"` with a fixed string drawn from the actual CLI output — e.g. `"Unavailable"` (capital-U, matching the metric output enum) — and note that the pre-existing `assert "unavailable" in text` in `test_user_guide_has_metrics_section` already covers lowercase, so the new assertion should target the install/setup prose (e.g. `"rebar[metrics]"` and `"PATH"`) rather than duplicating the fallback-phrase check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC1's PROVE grep with round-4 assertion (1) by adding `&& grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md` so the manual proof command matches what `test_metrics_surface_documented` will actually enforce.
evidence: AC1 PROVE: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md`
Round-4 assertion (1): `docs/reuse-surface.md` contains "[metrics]" AND "scc" AND "jscpd"
`jscpd` appears in assertion (1) but is absent from the AC1 grep command
findings.finding: AC1's PROVE grep command omits `jscpd`, making it weaker than the round-4 enumerated assertion (1), which requires `docs/reuse-surface.md` to contain `[metrics]` AND `scc` AND `jscpd`. An implementer following only the AC-level PROVE command could ship a reuse-surface.md that mentions `[metrics]` and `scc` but silently omits `jscpd`, pass the PROVE grep, yet fail the actual test assertion in `test_metrics_surface_documented`.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria, AC1 — PROVE command; Plan corrections round-4, assertion (1)
scenarios: Implementer writes reuse-surface.md with `[metrics]` and `scc` but omits `jscpd`; the AC1 grep passes; the test `test_metrics_surface_documented` fails — the AC and the test are inconsistent.
A reviewer spot-checking AC1 with the given grep concludes the deliverable is complete when it is not.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC1 PROVE line with: `grep -q "\[metrics\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.finding: AC2's proving command (`grep -q "\[code_health\]" docs/config.md`) only verifies the section header exists, not that every [code_health] key has a type and default documented — the gap between the stated criterion and the measurable proof is wide enough that the command can pass while the requirement is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grounded-facts line listing the [code_health] keys (discoverable from the config schema/source); update AC2's prove to e.g. `grep -q 'enabled' docs/config.md && grep -q '\[code_health\]' docs/config.md` using a key name that must appear.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1's PROVE command to also check for 'jscpd': append `&& grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`.
evidence: AC1 text: 'reuse-surface documents the analyzer seam + [metrics] extra (lizard) + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
PROVE command: `grep -Eq "metrics\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md` — 'jscpd' absent from grep chain
findings.finding: AC1's PROVE command does not check for 'jscpd' in docs/reuse-surface.md even though AC1's text requires jscpd to be documented there.
scenarios: Executor omits jscpd from reuse-surface.md; AC1's own PROVE command passes green; jscpd goes undocumented in that file

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's PROVE command to also assert the fallback phrase, e.g. append `&& grep -q "no analyzer" docs/user-guide.md`.
evidence: AC3 text: 'user-guide documents the three-part install + "no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior'
PROVE command only: `grep -q "rebar\[metrics\]" docs/user-guide.md`
findings.finding: AC3's PROVE command checks only for 'rebar[metrics]' but not for the Unavailable-fallback phrase, even though AC3 requires both parts ('three-part install + "no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior').
scenarios: Executor documents the install but omits the fallback behavior; AC3's PROVE passes green; the fallback contract goes undocumented in user-guide.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC1's PROVE command with both its criterion text and the Plan corrections assertion (1) by adding `&& grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md` to the PROVE command.
evidence: AC1 text: 'scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
AC1 PROVE: 'grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md' — no grep for jscpd
Plan corrections assertion (1): 'docs/reuse-surface.md contains "[metrics]" AND "scc" AND "jscpd"'
findings.finding: AC1's PROVE command only greps for 'scc' but AC1's own criterion text requires documenting 'scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs', and the Plan corrections (assertion 1) independently requires 'jscpd' to be present in reuse-surface.md — the PROVE command underspecifies what it must verify relative to both the criterion text and the corrections section.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC1 PROVE command vs. AC1 criterion text vs. Plan corrections assertion (1)
scenarios: An implementer who only reads AC1's PROVE passes CI without documenting jscpd, while the corrections-section test would fail — sending the implementer in two directions
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 PROVE to: `grep -Eq "metrics\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the AC1 PROVE command to also check for `jscpd`: e.g. `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`.
evidence: AC1 text: 'reuse-surface documents the analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra (lizard) + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
Plan corrections assertion (1): 'docs/reuse-surface.md contains "[metrics]" AND "scc" AND "jscpd"'
AC1 PROVE command contains only `grep -q "scc"` — jscpd is absent

Cache finding at .rebar/cache/plan-review/a9dd-041f-de89-48c5/1ef2e29bf9bd5a2eaad8.json already flagged this: 'AC1's PROVE command only greps for scc but AC1's own criterion text requires documenting scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
findings.finding: AC1's PROVE command omits a `jscpd` check, so it would give a false green for a reuse-surface.md that documents scc but silently omits jscpd. The AC1 criterion text explicitly requires 'scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs', and Plan corrections assertion (1) independently requires all three strings ('[metrics]', 'scc', 'jscpd'). A prior plan-review cache entry (a9dd…1ef2e29b) already flagged this exact gap, but the AC1 PROVE command was never updated.
findings.location: AC1 PROVE command: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md`
scenarios: An implementer writes reuse-surface.md that mentions scc but forgets jscpd; the AC1 PROVE command passes; the new test assertion (which does check jscpd) catches it at pytest-time instead — creating a disconnect between the observable proving command in the AC and the actual enforcement gate.
A reviewer checking AC1 compliance manually runs the PROVE command and concludes the criterion is satisfied even though jscpd is absent.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC1 PROVE command with: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.finding: AC1's proving command checks for 'scc' but not 'jscpd', even though the criterion explicitly requires both scc AND jscpd to be documented as PATH prereqs — the second prereq is entirely unverified by the grep.
findings.suggested_fix: PROVE: `grep -Eq 'metrics\]' docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q 'scc' docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q 'jscpd' docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.finding: AC1's PROVE command (`grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md`) only checks for `scc` but the criterion explicitly requires both `scc` AND `jscpd` to be documented as separate PATH prereqs. The enforcement mechanism silently passes if jscpd is omitted from the docs.
findings.suggested_fix: PROVE: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

evidence: verify command `head -1 docs/config.md` inspects output with `head` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Change AC4's PROVE command to run the full test file: `pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py` (covers both pre-existing 121b assertions and the new `test_metrics_surface_documented` function).
evidence: AC4 text: 'the pre-existing 121b assertions must still pass'
Proving command selects only `::test_metrics_surface_documented`, not the full module
findings.finding: AC4's proving command `pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py::test_metrics_surface_documented` runs only the new test function; it does NOT verify that pre-existing 121b assertions still pass, even though AC4 explicitly requires both.
scenarios: A change to the docs breaks a pre-existing test; AC4's proving command passes green because it skips the broken tests; the regression ships

findings.finding: AC4's PROVE command (`pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py`) passes trivially on the pre-existing 121b assertions without any new assertions being added, because none of the four existing test functions check for `[metrics]` extra presence in docs, scc/jscpd PATH-prereq text in reuse-surface.md, `[code_health]` key in config.md, or three-part install copy in user-guide.md. The plan says 'extend the existing docs test' but the prove command cannot distinguish zero new assertions from a full extension.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC4 / Approach section ('extend the existing docs test rather than adding a parallel one')

findings.finding: The test extension spec in AC4 does not enumerate what new assertions must be added to test_metrics_docs.py, leaving the verification step structurally unable to cover the new content deliverables in AC1+AC2 (docs/config.md [code_health] section, scc/jscpd as PATH prereqs in reuse-surface.md, lizard-only [metrics] extra, analyzer seam). The existing test file (confirmed by read) checks only the four 121b assertions; none of them touch config.md or the metrics extra/PATH prereqs. A passing test run after this ticket would NOT prove AC1+AC2 content is correct.
findings.location: AC4: 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended; existing assertions still pass' and Verification: 'test_metrics_docs.py passes (old + new assertions)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'New assertions must cover: (a) docs/config.md contains `[code_health]` and at least the keys `size_cap`/`size_near_fraction`; (b) docs/reuse-surface.md contains `scc` and `jscpd` as PATH prerequisites; (c) docs/reuse-surface.md names `lizard` as the only pip dep in the `[metrics]` extra; (d) docs/reuse-surface.md references the analyzer seam (AnalyzerResult or guard_import).'
